title: The Elevator Pitch
subtitle: 60 seconds. One investor. Make it count.

gameplay.setup: The lift doors close. Standing next to you is Meera Krishnan, an early-stage investor known for backing young founders. She smiles: 'So — what are you building?' You have until the 14th floor. Pitch your own idea — or pitch ChocoNation if you don't have one yet.

persona.name: Meera Krishnan
persona.background: Former operator who built a consumer brand before becoming an investor. Genuinely enjoys first-time founders and asks simple questions that turn out to be hard: who is it for, and why would they pay?
objections: Who exactly is your customer — can you describe one real person who'd want this?
How does this make money — walk me through one sale.

rubric.label: Clarity of the idea
rubric.description: A stranger could repeat back what the business does after one hearing. No jargon, one clear sentence for the problem and one for the solution.

rubric.label: Knows the customer
rubric.description: Names a specific customer (who, where, what situation) rather than 'everyone' or a vague demographic.

rubric.label: Path to money
rubric.description: Explains how one sale works: what the customer pays and roughly why they'd choose this.
